article_title: Johns Hopkins Digital Twin Hearts Achieve 100% Arrhythmia-Free Rate in First FDA-Approved Clinical Trial

content_preview.title: Johns Hopkins Digital Twin Hearts Achieve 100% Arrhythmia-Free Rate in First FDA-Approved Clinical Trial
content_preview.summary: The TWIN-VT trial used personalized computational heart models to guide ventricular tachycardia ablation, with all 10 patients remaining arrhythmia-free after more than a year.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Researchers at Johns Hopkins University have published results from the first FDA-approved clinical trial using digital twin technology to guide cardiac ablation procedures. The TWIN-VT trial, published on April 1, 2026 in the [New England Journal of Medicine](https://www.nejm.org/doi/full/10.1056/NEJMc2517822), demonstrated that all 10 patients with ventricular tachycardia remained arrhythmia-free more than a year after undergoing procedures planned on personalized computational re...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
2 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-written medical technology article with clear explanation of the digital twin approach, precise clinical results, and appropriate caveats about small sample size.
editor_notes.source_verification: News Medical fully accessible and confirms all key claims: 10 patients, 100% arrhythmia-free at 1+ year, 60% conventional rate, 3D contrast-enhanced MRI, 8/10 discontinued meds, Chrispin and Trayanova quotes, NEJM publication April 1 2026. MedicalXpress returned 404 and NEJM 403 (paywall) — but News Medical alone verifies all claims attributed to those sources via cross-corroboration.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified via News Medical. The Chrispin quote is slightly paraphrased in the article ('safer, shorter and more effective') vs the source ('safer, shorter') but the meaning is preserved. Trayanova's 'crowning achievement' quote confirmed.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Appropriately notes small sample size and scalability concerns.
editor_notes.originality: No prior coverage of cardiac digital twins or the TWIN-VT trial in the archive.
concerns: MedicalXpress URL returned 404 — link may have been updated or removed since article was written
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong medical technology article with verified claims from accessible source. NEJM paywall is expected. Approved.
